title: ## STANDARD FOR CERTAIN CANNED CITRUS FRUITS CXS 254-2007 Adopted in 2007. Amended in 2013, 2022, 2023.

sections.section: ## STANDARD FOR CERTAIN CANNED CITRUS FRUITS CXS 254-2007 Adopted in 2007. Amended in 2013, 2022, 2023.
sections.summary: Document title and administrative history: Standard for Certain Canned Citrus Fruits (CXS 254-2007), adopted 2007 and amended 2013, 2022, 2023.

States the standard applies to specified canned citrus fruits offered for direct consumption (including catering and repacking) and does not apply when the product is indicated as intended for further processing.

Describes canned citrus fruit product requirements: defines permitted fruit species, packing media and heat processing/cleaning requirements, specifies colour types for grapefruit/pummelo, and sets style definitions and rules for other presentations including labelling to avoid misleading consumers.

Defines canned citrus fruit as prepared from specified mature washed fruits (grapefruit, mandarin, sweet orange or pummelo), packed with water or suitable liquid/sugars/honey/spices, and heat-processed before or after hermetic sealing to prevent spoilage, with prior washing/peeling and substantial removal of membranes, seeds, core and albedo fibres.

sections.section: 2.2 Colour types (canned grapefruit or canned pummelo only)
sections.summary: Specifies colour types for canned grapefruit and pummelo as White (white-fleshed), Pink (pink or red-fleshed) and Pale yellow (pale yellow-fleshed pummelo).

Defines style criteria numerically (e.g., 'whole' = not less than 75% of original segment for most fruits, specific thresholds for pummelo and mandarin) and permits other distinctive presentations provided they meet relevant requirements (defect limits, drained weight) and are adequately described on the label to avoid consumer confusion.

Defines presentation styles (whole, broken, twin, pieces) for canned grapefruit, sweet orange and pummelo with quantitative size thresholds: whole segments must be at least 75% of the original segment for grapefruit and sweet orange, at least 50% for pummelo, 'broken' is less than those thresholds, and 'pieces' are defined by screen retention (8 mm2 openings formed by 2 mm wire).

sections.section: 2.3.2 Other styles (canned grapefruit, mandarin oranges, sweet orange varieties and pummelos)
sections.summary: Permits any other presentation style provided it is clearly distinctive from specified styles, meets all relevant standard requirements (defect limits, drained weight, etc.) matching the most similar style, and is adequately described on the label to avoid misleading consumers.

For canned mandarin oranges in whole-segment single-size packs, requires that in the 95% by count of the most size-uniform units (excluding broken segments) the largest unit weigh no more than twice the smallest unit.

For canned grapefruit, sweet orange varieties and pummelo sets defect limits: membrane surface ≤20 cm2 per 500 g, developed seeds ≤4 per 500 g (seed >9 mm), and blemished units ≤15% by weight of drained fruit; for canned mandarin oranges prescribes maximums by sample average: broken segments 10% (whole style) or 15% (twin), membrane 7 cm2/100 g, fibre strands 5 cm/100 g, and seeds >4 mm at 1/100 g.

Same as Section 3.2.6: establishes defect and allowance limits — for grapefruit, sweet orange varieties and pummelo (membrane ≤20 cm2/500 g; developed seeds ≤4/500 g; blemished units ≤15% by weight) and for mandarin oranges (broken segments, membrane, fibre and seed maxima based on sample averages as specified).

Defines a 'defective' container for canned grapefruit, mandarin oranges, sweet orange varieties and pummelo as any container failing one or more applicable quality requirements in Sections 2.4 and 3.2, excluding requirements based on sample averages.

A lot meets quality requirements when the number of 'defectives' does not exceed the sampling-plan acceptance number for an AQL of 6.5; additionally, lots of canned mandarin oranges must comply with the Section 3.2.6(b) requirements that are based on sample averages.

Permits acidity regulators and firming agents listed in Tables 1–3 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) for food category 04.1.2.4 (canned/pasteurized bottled fruit) to be used in products conforming to this standard.
Requires compliance with the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and with Codex-established maximum residue limits for pesticides.

Recommends preparing and handling products in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code for Low‑Acid and Acidified Low‑Acid Canned Foods (CXC 23-1979) and other relevant Codex texts, and states products should comply with any microbiological criteria established under CXG 21-1997.

Section heading covering weights and measures; sets minimum fill (90% of container water capacity minus necessary headspace), classifies containers failing minimum fill as 'defectives' with lot acceptance by an AQL 6.5 sampling plan, and specifies minimum drained-weight requirements by product plus lot-acceptance rules based on average drained weight.
Heading only for the 'Fill of container' subsection; no substantive provisions beyond the heading in this entry.

Requires the sealed container (including packing medium) to occupy not less than 90% of its water capacity (less any necessary headspace per GMP), where water capacity is the volume of distilled water at 20 °C the sealed container holds when completely filled.
States that any container failing the minimum-fill requirement of Section 7.1.1 shall be considered a 'defective'.
A lot complies with the minimum-fill requirement when the number of 'defectives' does not exceed the acceptance number (c) of the applicable sampling plan using an AQL of 6.5.

Sets minimum drained-weight requirements for different canned citrus (percentages specified by product) and provides that lot acceptance is based on the average drained weight meeting the minimum required so long as there is no unreasonable shortage in individual containers.

sections.section: 7.1.4.1 The minimum drained weight shall be as follows: iii
sections.summary: Specifies minimum drained-weight percentages: for canned grapefruit and sweet orange varieties (and pummelos in one clause) not less than 50% of the container's water‑capacity weight at 20 °C, for canned pummelo a 40% minimum, and for canned mandarin oranges a 56% minimum, all calculated on the basis of distilled water at 20 °C.

Provides lot-acceptance criteria for drained weight: the requirement is met when the average drained weight of examined containers is not less than the minimum required, provided there is no unreasonable shortage in individual containers.

Labelling must follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre‑packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) with specific provisions: the product name (grapefruit, mandarin oranges, pummelo or oranges) and, where applicable, style, packing medium and 'pink' colour type must appear with the name, flavour‑altering added ingredients must be declared as 'flavoured with X', mandarin labelling has additional style/size/count requirements, other styles must be clearly described to avoid consumer confusion, and non‑retail containers must comply with CXS 346-2021.

Requires the product name to be 'grapefruit', 'mandarin oranges', 'pummelo' or 'oranges' and mandates that style and packing medium appear with or close to the name (including 'pink' where applicable); if an added ingredient alters flavour, the label must state 'flavoured with X', and for mandarin oranges the style, optional size classification or unit count range, and packing medium must be declared, while other styles must include additional wording to avoid misleading consumers.

sections.section: 8.2.2 For canned grapefruit, sweet orange varieties, and canned pummelo
sections.summary: Specifies labelling requirements for canned grapefruit, sweet orange varieties, and canned pummelo: the product style and packing medium must be stated in or near the name, and if the fruit colour is 'pink' this colour type must be included; added ingredients that alter flavour trigger the flavoured declaration per 8.2.2.1.

sections.section: 8.2.2.1 If an added ingredient, as defined in Section 3.1.3, alters the flavour characteristic of the product, the name of the food shall be accompanied by the term 'flavoured with X' or 'X flavoured' as appropriate.
sections.summary: Requires that when an added ingredient alters the product's flavour, the product name be accompanied by the term 'flavoured with X' or 'X flavoured' as appropriate.

Requires canned mandarin oranges to declare style (e.g., whole segments or broken segments) adjacent to the product name, allows a size classification and a range count for whole segments if compliant with Section 2.4.1, permits size designation near the style, and requires the packing medium to be declared.

sections.section: 8.2.3.1 (i) whole segments - a size classification for whole segments style may be stated on the label if the pack complies with the appropriate requirements of Section 2.4.1 of this standard. In addition, the number of units present in the container may be shown by a range of count, e.g. '(number) to (number) whole segments'; and
sections.summary: Permits stating a size classification for 'whole segments' on the label if the pack meets the Section 2.4.1 requirements and allows indicating the number of units present by a range (e.g., 'X to Y whole segments').
Specifies 'broken segments' as an allowable declared style for canned mandarin oranges (labeling term).

sections.section: 8.2.4 Other styles - if the product is produced in accordance with the other styles provision (Section 2.3.2), the label should contain in close proximity to the name of the product such additional words or phrases that will avoid misleading or confusing the consumer.
sections.summary: States that products produced under the 'other styles' provision (Section 2.3.2) should include additional words or phrases near the product name to avoid misleading or confusing the consumer.
Requires labelling of non-retail containers to comply with the Codex General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

Mandates that methods of analysis and sampling used to check compliance with this standard shall be those in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the standard's provisions.
